They agreed to settle the dispute by seeing who could make the traveler take off his coat first. The north wind exerted all its strength and blew fiercely, but the harder it blew, the tighter the traveler clung to his coat. In the end, the north wind gave up in defeat. Soon afterward, the sun came out and shone warmly. The traveler immediately took off his coat. Thus, the north wind had to admit that the sun was indeed stronger.

The term "paleo" in "paleo diet" refers to the Paleolithic era, which is the period of human history spanning from approximately 2.5 million years ago to around 10,000 years ago. The paleo diet is based on the premise that modern humans are genetically adapted to the diet of their Paleolithic ancestors, and that consuming foods similar to those eaten during this period can promote better health and prevent chronic diseases. Proponents of the paleo diet argue that the shift to agriculture and the subsequent development of processed foods have led to a mismatch between our genetic makeup and contemporary dietary habits, contributing to the rise of conditions such as obesity, type 2 diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. By focusing on whole, unprocessed foods such as lean meats, fish, fruits, vegetables, nuts, and seeds—while avoiding grains, dairy, legumes, refined sugars, and processed foods—the paleo diet aims to align modern eating patterns with those of early humans.
